info.title: CVSS Re-Scorer Service
info.summary: Context-aware CVSS re-scoring (v3.0, v3.1, v4.0) driven by threat models
info.description:
The CVSS Re-Scorer automatically adjusts CVSS base scores (v3.0, v3.1, v4.0) by analysing security context extracted from a **Microsoft Threat Model** (.tm7) and a **CycloneDX SBOM**.

### Workflow

1. **Create a session** — identifies the application under analysis.
2. **Upload files** — threat model, SBOM (with optional embedded vulnerabilities), CVE data, and an analysis configuration.  Alternatively, use the single-shot *inventory* endpoint.
3. **Run analysis** — the pipeline re-scores each CVE, optionally performs attack-chain analysis, priority ranking, and what-if scenarios.
4. **Retrieve results** — download the unified HTML action report, JSON evidence, a CycloneDX VEX document, and the enriched SBOM.

### Key concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---|---|
| **Session** | An isolated workspace for one analysis run. |
| **Inventory mode** | Single multipart request that uploads a TM7 + SBOM, extracts CVEs, enriches components, and runs the full pipeline. |
| **Classic mode** | Upload each file individually, then trigger analysis. |
| **Rescoring rules** | YAML-driven rules that lower CVSS metrics when the threat model proves mitigating context (e.g. no inbound data path → AV:P). |
| **VEX** | CycloneDX Vulnerability Exploitability eXchange document carrying the adjusted scores. |

get.description: Return the allowlisted wizard methods available to API consumers.

post.description:
Call one allowlisted method from the wizard bridge.

This is the HTTP equivalent of ``window.pywebview.api.<method>(...)`` used
by the native desktop wizard. Arguments are passed as ``args`` / ``kwargs``
in the JSON body and the method return value is wrapped under ``result``.

get.description:
Return a path-free wizard view of uploaded session inputs.

This endpoint is intended for browser or integration consumers that stage
files through the normal session upload API and then need the wizard's
import-time context without operating on server-side team-directory paths.
Config writes and threat-model edits remain on the existing session
endpoints.
Return wizard metadata that does not require or expose local paths.

post.description:
Create an isolated session workspace for one application version.

All subsequent file uploads, configuration, and analysis runs are
scoped to this session.  A caller-supplied session ID may be
provided for idempotency; otherwise a UUID is generated.

get.description:
Return the current state of a session, including which files have
been uploaded and the analysis status.

delete.description:
Remove a session and all its data (uploads, cache, outputs) from
disk.  This operation is irreversible.

put.description:
Upload the Microsoft Threat Model (.tm7) file.

The threat model provides the security context (elements, data flows,
trust boundaries) used to evaluate whether CVSS metrics can be
lowered.

Upload a CycloneDX SBOM in JSON format.

Components should carry ``threatModelElementIds`` properties or
the pipeline will attempt name-based matching to the threat model.
If the SBOM includes a ``vulnerabilities`` section, a separate CVE
file upload is optional.

Upload a CVE data file (.json or .csv).

Optional when the CycloneDX SBOM already contains a
``vulnerabilities`` section.  Accepted formats:

- **JSON** — ``{"cves": [{"cve_id", "cvss_vector", ...}]}``

- **CSV** — exported from a vulnerability work-list.  Expected
  columns: ``cve_id``, ``cvss_vector``, ``base_score``, ``severity``
  (additional columns are ignored).

Upload an items.csv that maps components to threat-model elements.

Required columns: ``item`` (component name) and
``threatmodel_id`` (TM element GUID).  Additional columns are
ignored.

Upload a countermeasures YAML used by MITRE ATT&CK analysis.

The file maps ATT&CK mitigation IDs to coverage levels and
justifications.  Once uploaded it is used by both the standalone
``/mitre/analyze`` endpoint and, when ``mitre_enrichment`` is set,
by the re-scoring pipeline.

Upload a previously issued VEX document for drift validation.

Each call adds one file to the session's prior-VEX set; the
re-scoring run compares the new results against every uploaded prior
VEX statement when ``vex_validation`` is configured. Call repeatedly
to attach multiple prior VEX documents.

Upload a source-review verdict consumed by ``code_review.verdicts_dir``.

Each uploaded YAML is stored in a session-local verdict directory. During
analysis, the service injects that directory into ``config['code_review']``
so API users do not need pre-existing server-side paths.

Upload a standards compliance declaration for MITRE auto-mapping.

Uploaded declarations are stored under the session upload directory and
injected as ``compliance_dir`` / ``compliance_product_dir`` for standalone
MITRE analysis and combined report generation.

Set the analysis configuration as a JSON body.

Accepted top-level keys (the canonical analysis surface — see
:data:`cvss_rescorer.config_loader.ANALYSIS_KEYS`):

Required:
- **cyclonedx_mapping** — how SBOM components map to TM elements.
- **rescoring_rules** — per-metric rules that lower CVSS when
  context proves mitigation.

Context / environment:
- **trust_boundaries** — boundary definitions and default trust
  level.
- **cwe_mitigations** — CWE-specific mitigation overrides.
- **property_overrides** / **deployment_environment** — optional
  context hints.
- **component_evidence**, **critical_assets**, **critical_patching**,
  **ssvc**, **cwss**, **iec62443** — optional evidence and risk-policy
  context for the generated reports.

Engine knobs:
- **rescoring_policy** — e.g. ``missing_cwe: skip|generic_only``.
- **vex_validation**, **review_skeptic**, **code_review**,
  **code_review_prompts**, **mitre_countermeasures**,
  **compliance_dir**, **compliance_product_dir**.

L6 evidentiary controls:
- **compliance_mode** (``none|audit_ready|qmsr|medical``), **product**,
  **device**, **audit**, **archives**, **drift**, **pipeline_version**,
  **traceability**, **sbom_quality**, **kev**, **kev_cves**,
  **safety_signoff**, **postmarket_lifecycle**, **patch_sla**,
  **safety_impact**, **safety_impact_by_element**, **safety_matrix**,
  **safety_review**, **mitigation_evidence**,
  **evidence_backed_mitigations**, **customer_assumption_accepted**.

Unknown keys are silently dropped.

schema.description: Analysis configuration as a JSON object
schema.title: Config

put.description:
Upload analysis configuration as a YAML file.

The file should contain only analysis sections (rescoring_rules,
trust_boundaries, etc.) — input/output/cache paths are ignored.

post.description:
Trigger the CVSS re-scoring pipeline using previously uploaded files.

**Prerequisites:** threat model, SBOM, and analysis config must
already be uploaded.  CVE data is optional — when absent,
vulnerabilities are extracted from the SBOM's ``vulnerabilities``
section.

The call blocks until the pipeline finishes for backward compatibility.
The analysis task itself is shielded from caller disconnects, so results
can still be retrieved later. For long online NVD runs, prefer
``POST /analyze/start`` to return immediately and poll ``GET /progress``.

post.summary: Start analysis in the background (classic mode)
post.description:
Start analysis without holding the HTTP response open.

Use this endpoint for large online NVD enrichments. Poll the returned
``progress_url`` until the status is terminal, then retrieve
``results_url``. Only the request connection is decoupled; the same shared
pipeline, cache, offline behavior, and scoring semantics are used.

post.summary: Upload & analyse in one step (inventory mode)
post.description:
One-shot analysis: upload or reuse a TM7 threat model and CycloneDX SBOM
(with embedded ``vulnerabilities``), then receive the re-scored results.

The SBOM components are automatically enriched with
``threatModelElementIds`` properties linking each component to the
matching threat-model elements.  The enriched SBOM is available as
an output file after analysis completes.

On the first run, both TM7 and SBOM must be provided. Later calls in the
same session may upload only the changed input, allowing a new threat model
to be analyzed against the stored SBOM. If no analysis configuration is
provided, the previous session config is reused; otherwise sensible defaults
(name-based component-to-element matching, standard rescoring rules) are
used. Set the ``background=true`` query parameter for large online NVD
runs; uploads and input preparation finish before an immediate ``202`` is
returned, then callers poll the normal progress and results endpoints.

schema.title: Response Analyze Inventory Api V1 Sessions  Session Id  Inventory Post

get.description:
Return the summary of the last completed analysis, including CVE
counts, average score reduction, elapsed time, and download links
for every output artefact.

Download the full rescoring results as a JSON document containing
original and adjusted scores for every CVE.

Download the Vulnerability Exploitability eXchange (VEX) document
in CycloneDX JSON format.  The VEX carries the re-scored CVSS
vectors and justification annotations.

Download a specific output artefact by filename.

``rescore_report.html`` is the primary human report. Available files
depend on the analysis options selected and may also include machine
outputs and text sidecars such as ``rescore_results.json``,
``rescore_vex.cdx.json``, ``priority_report.txt``,
``attack_chain_analysis.txt``, and ``enriched_sbom.cdx.json``.
Return missing threat-model properties/threat dispositions with context.

patch.description: Apply user-selected property/threat fixes to the stored TM7 file.

get.description: Download the current stored TM7 file, including any editor changes.

post.description:
Grade the uploaded threat model (.tm7) against the vscorer / FDA /
GEHC input-quality rule catalogue.

Returns an A–F grade, per-family findings, and actionable fix hints.
Run this before ``/analyze`` to catch missing properties, threats,
or trust boundaries that would weaken the re-scoring result.

Grade the uploaded CycloneDX SBOM against CycloneDX / CISA minimum
and FDA expectations, plus vscorer component-matching quality.

Grade the uploaded CVE data file for required fields, coverage, and
vscorer rescoring quality (e.g. presence of CVSS vectors and CWEs).

get.description:
Run every applicable validator (threat model, SBOM, CVE list) for
the files uploaded so far and return a combined report.

At least one input file must be uploaded.

get.summary: Export validation findings as a Markdown TODO list
get.description:
Render the findings from every applicable validator as a Markdown
TODO checklist that can be pasted into an issue tracker.

get.summary: List trust boundaries in the threat model
get.description:
List the trust-boundary names defined in the uploaded threat model.

Use one of these names as ``trust_boundary`` when calling
``POST /mitre/analyze``.

post.summary: Run standalone MITRE ATT&CK boundary analysis
post.description:
Run MITRE ATT&CK trust-boundary analysis for a single boundary.

Maps in-scope ATT&CK techniques to threat-model coverage, applies any
uploaded countermeasures (and compliance-derived auto-mappings), and
generates a heatmap report retrievable via ``GET /mitre/heatmap``.

When ``use_rescore_results`` is true and a prior analysis has run in
this session, unmitigated CVEs degrade technique coverage.

get.summary: Download the MITRE ATT&CK heatmap report
get.description: Download the HTML heatmap produced by ``POST /mitre/analyze``.

Return the medical-mode / L6 evidentiary gate decisions from the last
analysis run.

Gates only run when ``compliance_mode`` is set to ``medical`` in the
analysis configuration. Each entry reports whether the gate blocked the
run, any warnings, details, and the names of artefacts it produced
(retrievable via ``/outputs/{name}``).

List every artefact in the session output directory with its size and
download URL.

Covers all generated files — HTML report, JSON, VEX, priority and chain
reports, MITRE heatmap, audit trail, drift report, run archive, SRA
export, medical-gate artefacts, and explanation files.
Return non-secret operational status for UI and startup checks.

Return the current pipeline step, total steps, and a human-readable
message.  Intended for polling while an analysis is running.

progress_url.title: Progress Url
progress_url.description: URL to poll for live progress.

AnalysisAccepted.title: AnalysisAccepted
AnalysisAccepted.description: Immediate response for a background analysis start.

chain_analysis.title: Chain Analysis
chain_analysis.description: Run attack-chain analysis across linked components.

clear_cache.title: Clear Cache
clear_cache.description: Discard cached EPSS/KEV data and re-fetch.

enrich.title: Enrich
enrich.description: Run LLM-based threat-justification enrichment before analysis (requires a configured LLM backend).

ollama_model.title: Ollama Model
ollama_model.description: LLM model to use for enrichment (only relevant when enrich=True).

mitre_enrichment.title: Mitre Enrichment
mitre_enrichment.description: Enrich re-scored CVEs with MITRE ATT&CK countermeasure coverage (degrades technique coverage for unmitigated CVEs).

mitre_countermeasures_path.title: Mitre Countermeasures Path
mitre_countermeasures_path.description: Optional server-side path to a countermeasures YAML. When omitted, a countermeasures file uploaded to this session (or config['mitre_countermeasures']) is used.

offline.title: Offline
offline.description: Cache-only run: skip all NVD/KEV/EPSS network calls and use locally cached threat intel only.

AnalysisRequest.title: AnalysisRequest
AnalysisRequest.description: Options for triggering an analysis run.

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: The session that produced this result.

rescored_count.title: Rescored Count
rescored_count.description: Number of CVEs whose score was lowered.

avg_score_reduction.title: Avg Score Reduction
avg_score_reduction.description: Mean CVSS score reduction across rescored CVEs.

elapsed_seconds.title: Elapsed Seconds
elapsed_seconds.description: Wall-clock time for the pipeline run.

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Map of output name to download URL path.

AnalysisResult.title: AnalysisResult
AnalysisResult.description: Summary returned after an analysis run completes.

threatmodel.title: Threatmodel
threatmodel.description: Microsoft Threat Model (.tm7). Optional on rerun when the session already has one.

sbom.title: Sbom
sbom.description: CycloneDX SBOM with vulnerabilities (.json). Optional on rerun when the session already has one.

items_csv.title: Items Csv
items_csv.description: Optional items.csv mapping components to TM elements.

config.title: Config
config.description: Optional analysis config (.yaml). Uses defaults if omitted.

chain_analysis.title: Chain Analysis
chain_analysis.description: Run attack-chain analysis across linked components.

enrich.title: Enrich
enrich.description: Run LLM-based threat-justification enrichment before analysis (requires a configured LLM backend).

ollama_model.title: Ollama Model
ollama_model.description: LLM model for enrichment (only when enrich=True).

mitre_enrichment.title: Mitre Enrichment
mitre_enrichment.description: Enrich re-scored CVEs with MITRE ATT&CK countermeasure coverage (uses a countermeasures file uploaded to this session).

offline.title: Offline
offline.description: Cache-only run: skip all NVD/KEV/EPSS network calls.

product_overlay.title: Product Overlay
product_overlay.description: Store as a product-specific overlay instead of a baseline declaration.

file.title: File
file.description: CSV mapping components to threat-model elements (.csv)

trust_boundary.title: Trust Boundary
trust_boundary.description: Name of the trust boundary (from the threat model) to analyse.

countermeasures_path.title: Countermeasures Path
countermeasures_path.description: Optional server-side path to a countermeasures YAML. When omitted, a countermeasures file uploaded to this session (or config['mitre_countermeasures']) is used.

use_rescore_results.title: Use Rescore Results
use_rescore_results.description: Feed the last analysis's re-scored CVEs into the boundary analysis for CVE-driven exposure degradation (if available).

MitreAnalysisRequest.title: MitreAnalysisRequest
MitreAnalysisRequest.description: Options for a standalone MITRE ATT&CK trust-boundary analysis.

application_name.title: Application Name
application_name.description: Name of the application under analysis

session_id.title: Session Id
session_id.description: Caller-supplied session identifier. If omitted a UUID is generated.

SessionCreate.title: SessionCreate
SessionCreate.description: Payload for creating a new analysis session.

application_name.title: Application Name
application_name.description: Name of the application under analysis.

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Stable hash derived from application name and version.

has_threat_model.title: Has Threat Model
has_threat_model.description: Whether a threat model (.tm7) has been uploaded.

has_sbom.title: Has Sbom
has_sbom.description: Whether a CycloneDX SBOM has been uploaded.

has_cves.title: Has Cves
has_cves.description: Whether a CVE data file has been uploaded.

has_items_csv.title: Has Items Csv
has_items_csv.description: Whether an items.csv mapping file has been uploaded.

has_countermeasures.title: Has Countermeasures
has_countermeasures.description: Whether MITRE countermeasures have been uploaded.

prior_vex_count.title: Prior Vex Count
prior_vex_count.description: Number of prior VEX documents uploaded for this session.

code_review_verdict_count.title: Code Review Verdict Count
code_review_verdict_count.description: Number of code-review verdict YAML files uploaded.

compliance_declaration_count.title: Compliance Declaration Count
compliance_declaration_count.description: Number of compliance declaration YAML files uploaded.

has_analysis_config.title: Has Analysis Config
has_analysis_config.description: Whether an analysis configuration has been set.

status.title: Status
status.description: Current session status: created, ready, running, completed, or failed.

issue_id.title: Issue Id
issue_id.description: Issue identifier from the editor state.

action.title: Action
action.description: 'set' to apply values or 'keep' to leave missing.

target_id.title: Target Id
target_id.description: TM7 object GUID or threat ID.

note.title: Note
note.description: Optional reason when keeping a missing value.

ThreatModelEditorPatch.title: ThreatModelEditorPatch
ThreatModelEditorPatch.description: Single user decision from the threat-model repair editor.

args.title: Args
args.description: Positional arguments passed to the allowlisted wizard method.

kwargs.title: Kwargs
kwargs.description: Keyword arguments passed to the allowlisted wizard method.

WizardCallRequest.title: WizardCallRequest
WizardCallRequest.description: Generic call payload for the service-hosted configuration wizard API.

tags.name: Sessions
tags.description: Create, inspect, and delete analysis sessions. A session groups all inputs, configuration, and outputs for a single application version.

tags.name: File uploads
tags.description: Upload individual input files (threat model, SBOM, CVE data, items.csv) into an existing session. Used in *classic* mode.

tags.name: Configuration
tags.description: Set or upload the analysis configuration (rescoring rules, trust boundaries, CWE mitigations, etc.).

tags.name: Analysis
tags.description: Trigger re-scoring analysis and monitor progress. Supports both classic (POST /analyze) and inventory (POST /inventory) modes.

tags.name: Results
tags.description: Retrieve analysis results: summary, JSON details, CycloneDX VEX, the unified HTML action report, and the enriched SBOM.

tags.name: Validators
tags.description: Pre-flight input-quality validation. Grade an uploaded threat model, SBOM, or CVE list against vscorer / FDA / CISA guidance and export a Markdown TODO list of fixes.

tags.name: MITRE ATT&CK
tags.description: Standalone MITRE ATT&CK trust-boundary analysis, countermeasure uploads, and heatmap retrieval.

tags.name: Compliance
tags.description: Medical-mode / L6 evidentiary gate results and the full list of generated output artefacts.

tags.name: Wizard
tags.description: Service-hosted configuration-wizard operations. These endpoints expose the same YAML authoring and validation bridge used by the native wizard for API consumers.
